- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.

QUERY:
Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.

## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

transfer_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'transfer_count']

# Also need to identify all agents who handled cases (transferred to or initially assigned)

# Get initial assignments (first owners)

initial_assignments = [clean_id(x) for x in initial_assignments if x is not None and x != '']

# Get subsequent owners (transferred to)
subsequent_owners = df[df['is_transfer']]['newvalue__c'].unique()
subsequent_owners = [clean_id(x) for x in subsequent_owners if x is not None and x != '']

# Get agents who transferred away (these may not necessarily appear as initial or subsequent)
transferring_agents = df[df['is_transfer']]['oldvalue__c'].unique()
transferring_agents = [clean_id(x) for x in transferring_agents if x is not None and x != '']

# Combine all agents who handled cases
all_handling_agents = set(initial_assignments) | set(subsequent_owners) | set(transferring_agents)
all_handling_agents.discard('')  # Remove empty string if present
all_handling_agents.discard('None')  # Remove 'None' string if present

# Filter out agents with 0 cases handled

# Count initial assignments per agent
initial_counts = df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')]

initial_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'initial_count']

# Count subsequent assignments (transferred to) per agent
subsequent_counts = df[df['is_transfer']].groupby('newvalue__c').size().reset_index(name='subsequent_count')
subsequent_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'subsequent_count']

# Combine counts

# Filter agents with > 0 cases
df['caseid__c'] = df['caseid__c'].astype(str)
case_counts['agent_id'] = case_counts['agent_id'].astype(str)

# Count unique cases per agent (both initial and subsequent)

# Initial assignments
for idx, row in df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
    cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Subsequent assignments
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
    cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Also add agents who only transferred away (they may have never been assigned to in our dataset)
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['oldvalue__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        # We don't actually know what cases this agent handled, just that they transferred at least one
        # For the purpose of "handled more than 0 cases", we need to verify if they handled any
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()

for agent, cases in cases_by_agent.items():

    {'agent_id': agent, 'case_count': len(cases)} 
    for agent, cases in cases_by_agent_clean.items()
)

# Merge with active agents

# Fill NaN transfer counts with 0
result_df['transfer_count'] = result_df['transfer_count'].fillna(0)

# Sort by transfer count and get the agent with the fewest transfers
result_df = result_df.sort_values(['transfer_count', 'agent_id'])

fewest_transfers_agent = result_df.iloc[0]

# Prepare result to include full analysis for debugging
result = {
    'agent_with_fewest_transfers': fewest_transfers_agent['agent_id'],
    'transfer_count': int(fewest_transfers_agent['transfer_count']),
    'cases_handled': int(fewest_transfers_agent['case_count']),
    'total_agents_active': len(active_agents)
}

print('__RESULT__:')

{"code": "import json
import pandas as pd

# Load the owner assignment data assignments = var_functions.query_db:8

# Create a DataFrame for easier processing
df = pd.DataFrame(assignments)

# Clean up potential corruption in IDs
def clean_id(id_val):
    if id_val is None:
        return None
    id_str = str(id_val)
    # Remove leading # if present
    if id_str.startswith('#'):
        id_str = id_str[1:]
    # Trim whitespace
    return id_str.strip()

# Apply cleanup to relevant columns
for col in ['caseid__c', 'oldvalue__c', 'newvalue__c']:
    df[col] = df[col].apply(clean_id)

# Identify transfers: oldvalue is not None and not 'None' and different from newvalue
df['is_transfer'] = df['oldvalue__c'].notna() & (df['oldvalue__c'] != 'None') & (df['oldvalue__c'] != '')

# Count unique cases per agent (both initial and subsequent)
cases_by_agent = {}

# Initial assignments
for idx, row in df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent and agent != 'None' and agent != '':
        if agent not in cases_by_agent:
            cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
        cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Subsequent assignments (transferred to)
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent and agent != 'None' and agent != '':
        if agent not in cases_by_agent:
            cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
        cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Count per agent
case_count_list = []
for agent, cases in cases_by_agent.items():
    case_count_list.append({'agent_id': agent, 'case_count': len(cases)})

case_count_df = pd.DataFrame(case_count_list)

# Filter agents with more than 0 cases
active_agents = case_count_df[case_count_df['case_count'] > 0]

# Count transfers: when agent is the oldvalue (transferring FROM)
transfer_counts = df[df['is_transfer']]['oldvalue__c'].value_counts().reset_index()
transfer_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'transfer_count']

# Merge with active agents
result_df = pd.merge(active_agents, transfer_counts, on='agent_id', how='left')

# Fill NaN transfer counts with 0
result_df['transfer_count'] = result_df['transfer_count'].fillna(0)

# Sort by transfer count and get the agent with the fewest transfers
result_df = result_df.sort_values(['transfer_count', 'agent_id'])

if len(result_df) > 0:
    fewest_transfers_agent = result_df.iloc[0]
    result = {
        'agent_with_fewest_transfers': fewest_transfers_agent['agent_id'],
        'transfer_count': int(fewest_transfers_agent['transfer_count']),
        'cases_handled': int(fewest_transfers_agent['case_count']),
        'total_agents_active': len(active_agents)
    }
else:
    result = {'error': 'No agents found with more than 0 cases'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))"}

QUERY:
Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.

## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-04-10

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent

     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.

    id_str = str(id_val)
    # Remove leading # if present
    if id_str.startswith('#'):
        id_str = id_str[1:]
    # Trim whitespace
    return id_str.strip()

# Apply cleanup to relevant columns
for col in ['caseid__c', 'oldvalue__c', 'newvalue__c']:
    df[col] = df[col].apply(clean_id)

# Count transfers per agent (transferred FROM this agent)
transfer_counts = df[df['is_transfer']]['oldvalue__c'].value_counts().reset_index()
transfer_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'transfer_count']

# Also need to identify all agents who handled cases (transferred to or initially assigned)
# This includes both oldvalue and newvalue where oldvalue is not 'None'
# and all newvalue where oldvalue is 'None'

# Get initial assignments (first owners)
initial_assignments = df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')]['newvalue__c'].unique()
initial_assignments = [clean_id(x) for x in initial_assignments if x is not None and x != '']

# Get subsequent owners (transferred to)
subsequent_owners = df[df['is_transfer']]['newvalue__c'].unique()
subsequent_owners = [clean_id(x) for x in subsequent_owners if x is not None and x != '']

# Get agents who transferred away (these may not necessarily appear as initial or subsequent)
transferring_agents = df[df['is_transfer']]['oldvalue__c'].unique()
transferring_agents = [clean_id(x) for x in transferring_agents if x is not None and x != '']

# Combine all agents who handled cases
all_handling_agents = set(initial_assignments) | set(subsequent_owners) | set(transferring_agents)
all_handling_agents.discard('')  # Remove empty string if present
all_handling_agents.discard('None')  # Remove 'None' string if present

# Filter out agents with 0 cases handled
# We need to count cases per agent - both initial assignments and cases transferred to them

# Count initial assignments per agent
initial_counts = df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')]
initial_counts = initial_counts.groupby('newvalue__c').size().reset_index(name='initial_count')
initial_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'initial_count']

# Count subsequent assignments (transferred to) per agent
subsequent_counts = df[df['is_transfer']].groupby('newvalue__c').size().reset_index(name='subsequent_count')
subsequent_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'subsequent_count']

# Combine counts
case_counts = pd.merge(initial_counts, subsequent_counts, on='agent_id', how='outer')
case_counts['total_cases'] = case_counts['initial_count'].fillna(0) + case_counts['subsequent_count'].fillna(0)

# Filter agents with > 0 cases
df['caseid__c'] = df['caseid__c'].astype(str)
case_counts['agent_id'] = case_counts['agent_id'].astype(str)

# Count unique cases per agent (both initial and subsequent)
cases_by_agent = {}

# Initial assignments
for idx, row in df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
    cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Subsequent assignments
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
    cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Also add agents who only transferred away (they may have never been assigned to in our dataset)
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['oldvalue__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        # We don't actually know what cases this agent handled, just that they transferred at least one
        # For the purpose of "handled more than 0 cases", we need to verify if they handled any
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()

# Filter out invalid IDs
cases_by_agent_clean = {}
for agent, cases in cases_by_agent.items():
    if agent and agent != 'None' and agent != '':
        cases_by_agent_clean[agent] = cases

# Create DataFrame with case counts
case_count_df = pd.DataFrame(
    {'agent_id': agent, 'case_count': len(cases)} 
    for agent, cases in cases_by_agent_clean.items()
)

# Filter agents with more than 0 cases
active_agents = case_count_df[case_count_df['case_count'] > 0]

# Add transfer counts
transfer_counts['agent_id'] = transfer_counts['agent_id'].astype(str)

# Merge with active agents
result_df = pd.merge(active_agents, transfer_counts, on='agent_id', how='left')

# Fill NaN transfer counts with 0
result_df['transfer_count'] = result_df['transfer_count'].fillna(0)

# Sort by transfer count and get the agent with the fewest transfers
result_df = result_df.sort_values(['transfer_count', 'agent_id'])

fewest_transfers_agent = result_df.iloc[0]

# Prepare result to include full analysis for debugging
result = {
    'agent_with_fewest_transfers': fewest_transfers_agent['agent_id'],
    'transfer_count': int(fewest_transfers_agent['transfer_count']),
    'cases_handled': int(fewest_transfers_agent['case_count']),
    'total_agents_active': len(active_agents)
}

print('__RESULT__:')

QUERY:
Identify the agent with the fewest transfer counts in the last 4 quarters among those who handled more than 0 cases. Return only the Id of the agent.

## Transfer Count Policy
- Definition: The number of instances a case was reassigned or transferred from one agent to another. Each transfer from agent A to agent B adds to the transfer count for agent A.
- In the queries that specify 'agents managed/queries x cases' -- this filter applies to both the first agent that the case was first assigned to and the agent that the case was transferred to. This means that if an agent has 2 cases that was initially assigned to itself by admin and 1 case transferred from another agent, a filter like 'handled/managed at least 3 cases' would not filter this agent out.
- For cases that have NOT been transferred to an other agent, there will be only ONE 'Owner Assignment', and for those that have been transferred, there will be MORE THAN ONE 'Owner Assignment'.

## Today's date: 2023-04-10

DATABASE DESCRIPTION:
You are working with six databases to solve this query.

Here are the descriptions of these six databases:

1. core_crm
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains core CRM data including users, accounts, and contacts from Salesforce CRMArena.
   - This database consists of three tables:
    - User: Sales team information
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Username, Alias, LanguageLocaleKey, EmailEncodingKey, TimeZoneSidKey, LocaleSidKey
    - Account: Company/customer data
      - Fields: Id, Name, Phone, Industry, Description, NumberOfEmployees, ShippingState
    - Contact: Individual contacts
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, AccountId

2. sales_pipeline
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains sales pipeline data including opportunities, quotes, contracts, and leads.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Contract: Signed contracts
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, StartDate, CustomerSignedDate, CompanySignedDate, Description, ContractTerm
     - Lead: Sales leads
      - Fields: Id, FirstName, LastName, Email, Phone, Company, Status, ConvertedContactId, ConvertedAccountId, Title, CreatedDate, ConvertedDate, IsConverted, OwnerId
     - Opportunity: Sales deals
      - Fields: Id, ContractID__c, AccountId, ContactId, OwnerId, Probability, Amount, StageName, Name, Description, CreatedDate, CloseDate
     - OpportunityLineItem: Deal line items
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, TotalPrice
     - Quote: Price quotes
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId, AccountId, ContactId, Name, Description, Status, CreatedDate, ExpirationDate
     - QuoteLineItem: Quote details
      - Fields: Id, QuoteId, OpportunityLineItemId, Product2Id, PricebookEntryId, Quantity, UnitPrice, Discount, TotalPrice

3. support
   - This database is stored in a PostgreSQL database and contains customer support data including cases, knowledge articles, and communications.
   - This database consists of six tables:
     - Case: Support cases
      - Fields: id, priority, subject, description, status, contactid, createddate, closeddate, orderitemid__c, issueid__c, accountid, ownerid
     - knowledge__kav: Knowledge articles
      - Fields: id, title, faq_answer__c, summary, urlname
     - issue__c: Custom issues
      - Fields: id, name, description__c
     - casehistory__c: Case history
      - Fields: id, caseid__c, oldvalue__c, newvalue__c, createddate, field__c
     - emailmessage: Email communications
      - Fields: id, subject, textbody, parentid, fromaddress, toids, messagedate, relatedtoid
     - livechattranscript: Chat logs
      - Fields: id, caseid, accountid, ownerid, body, endtime, livechatvisitorid, contactid

4. products_orders
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains product catalog and order data.
   - This database consists of seven tables:
     - ProductCategory: Product categories
      - Fields: Id, Name, CatalogId
     - Product2: Product catalog
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, External_ID__c
     - ProductCategoryProduct: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, ProductCategoryId, ProductId
     - Pricebook2: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description, IsActive, ValidFrom, ValidTo
     - PricebookEntry: one of the pricing and mapping tables
      - Fields: Id, Pricebook2Id, Product2Id, UnitPrice
     - Order: Customer orders
      - Fields: Id, AccountId, Status, EffectiveDate, Pricebook2Id, OwnerId
     - OrderItem: Order details
      - Fields: Id, OrderId, Product2Id, Quantity, UnitPrice, PriceBookEntryId

5. activities
   - This database is stored in a DuckDB database and contains activity data including tasks, events, and voice call transcripts.
   - This database consists of three tables:
     - Event: Calendar events
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, StartDateTime, Subject, Description, DurationInMinutes, Location, IsAllDayEvent
     - Task: Activities and tasks
      - Fields: Id, WhatId, OwnerId, Priority, Status, ActivityDate, Subject, Description
     - VoiceCallTranscript__c: Call records
      - Fields: Id, OpportunityId__c, LeadId__c, Body__c, CreatedDate, EndTime__c

6. territory
   - This database is stored in a SQLite database and contains sales territory management data.
   - This database consists of two tables:
     - Territory2: Sales territories
      - Fields: Id, Name, Description
     - UserTerritory2Association: Territory assignments
      - Id, UserId, Territory2Id

- Corruption may appear in: Id, AccountId, ContactId, Name, FirstName, LastName, Email, Subject, Status.

{"code": "import json
import pandas as pd

# Load the owner assignment data
owner_assignments = var_functions.query_db:8

# Create a DataFrame for easier processing
df = pd.DataFrame(owner_assignments)

# Clean up potential corruption in IDs
def clean_id(id_val):
    if id_val is None:
        return None
    id_str = str(id_val)
    # Remove leading # if present
    if id_str.startswith('#'):
        id_str = id_str[1:]
    # Trim whitespace
    return id_str.strip()

# Apply cleanup to relevant columns
for col in ['caseid__c', 'oldvalue__c', 'newvalue__c']:
    df[col] = df[col].apply(clean_id)

# Identify transfers: oldvalue is not None and not 'None' and different from newvalue
df['is_transfer'] = df['oldvalue__c'].notna() & (df['oldvalue__c'] != 'None') & (df['oldvalue__c'] != '')

# Count transfers per agent (transferred FROM this agent)
transfer_counts = df[df['is_transfer']]['oldvalue__c'].value_counts().reset_index()
transfer_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'transfer_count']

# Also need to identify all agents who handled cases (transferred to or initially assigned)
# This includes both oldvalue and newvalue where oldvalue is not 'None'
# and all newvalue where oldvalue is 'None'

# Get initial assignments (first owners)
initial_assignments = df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')]['newvalue__c'].unique()
initial_assignments = [clean_id(x) for x in initial_assignments if x is not None and x != '']

# Get subsequent owners (transferred to)
subsequent_owners = df[df['is_transfer']]['newvalue__c'].unique()
subsequent_owners = [clean_id(x) for x in subsequent_owners if x is not None and x != '']

# Get agents who transferred away (these may not necessarily appear as initial or subsequent)
transferring_agents = df[df['is_transfer']]['oldvalue__c'].unique()
transferring_agents = [clean_id(x) for x in transferring_agents if x is not None and x != '']

# Combine all agents who handled cases
all_handling_agents = set(initial_assignments) | set(subsequent_owners) | set(transferring_agents)
all_handling_agents.discard('')  # Remove empty string if present
all_handling_agents.discard('None')  # Remove 'None' string if present

# Filter out agents with 0 cases handled
# We need to count cases per agent - both initial assignments and cases transferred to them

# Count initial assignments per agent
initial_counts = df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')]
initial_counts = initial_counts.groupby('newvalue__c').size().reset_index(name='initial_count')
initial_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'initial_count']

# Count subsequent assignments (transferred to) per agent
subsequent_counts = df[df['is_transfer']].groupby('newvalue__c').size().reset_index(name='subsequent_count')
subsequent_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'subsequent_count']

# Combine counts
case_counts = pd.merge(initial_counts, subsequent_counts, on='agent_id', how='outer')
case_counts['total_cases'] = case_counts['initial_count'].fillna(0) + case_counts['subsequent_count'].fillna(0)

# Filter agents with > 0 cases
df['caseid__c'] = df['caseid__c'].astype(str)
case_counts['agent_id'] = case_counts['agent_id'].astype(str)

# Count unique cases per agent (both initial and subsequent)
cases_by_agent = {}

# Initial assignments
for idx, row in df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
    cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Subsequent assignments
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
    cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Also add agents who only transferred away (they may have never been assigned to in our dataset)
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['oldvalue__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        # We don't actually know what cases this agent handled, just that they transferred at least one
        # For the purpose of "handled more than 0 cases", we need to verify if they handled any
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()

# Filter out invalid IDs
cases_by_agent_clean = {}
for agent, cases in cases_by_agent.items():
    if agent and agent != 'None' and agent != '':
        cases_by_agent_clean[agent] = cases

# Create DataFrame with case counts
case_count_df = pd.DataFrame(
    {'agent_id': agent, 'case_count': len(cases)} 
    for agent, cases in cases_by_agent_clean.items()
)

# Filter agents with more than 0 cases
active_agents = case_count_df[case_count_df['case_count'] > 0]

# Add transfer counts
transfer_counts['agent_id'] = transfer_counts['agent_id'].astype(str)

# Merge with active agents
result_df = pd.merge(active_agents, transfer_counts, on='agent_id', how='left')

# Fill NaN transfer counts with 0
result_df['transfer_count'] = result_df['transfer_count'].fillna(0)

# Sort by transfer count and get the agent with the fewest transfers
result_df = result_df.sort_values(['transfer_count', 'agent_id'])

fewest_transfers_agent = result_df.iloc[0]

# Prepare result to include full analysis for debugging
result = {
    'agent_with_fewest_transfers': fewest_transfers_agent['agent_id'],
    'transfer_count': int(fewest_transfers_agent['transfer_count']),
    'cases_handled': int(fewest_transfers_agent['case_count']),
    'total_agents_active': len(active_agents)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))"}

{"code": "import json
import pandas as pd

# Load the owner assignment data
owner_assignments = var_functions.query_db:8

# Create a DataFrame for easier processing
df = pd.DataFrame(owner_assignments)

# Clean up potential corruption in IDs
def clean_id(id_val):
    if id_val is None:
        return None
    id_str = str(id_val)
    # Remove leading # if present
    if id_str.startswith('#'):
        id_str = id_str[1:]
    # Trim whitespace
    return id_str.strip()

# Apply cleanup to relevant columns
for col in ['caseid__c', 'oldvalue__c', 'newvalue__c']:
    df[col] = df[col].apply(clean_id)

# Identify transfers: oldvalue is not None and not 'None' and different from newvalue
df['is_transfer'] = df['oldvalue__c'].notna() & (df['oldvalue__c'] != 'None') & (df['oldvalue__c'] != '')

# Count transfers per agent (transferred FROM this agent)
transfer_counts = df[df['is_transfer']]['oldvalue__c'].value_counts().reset_index()
transfer_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'transfer_count']

# Also need to identify all agents who handled cases (transferred to or initially assigned)
# This includes both oldvalue and newvalue where oldvalue is not 'None'
# and all newvalue where oldvalue is 'None'

# Get initial assignments (first owners)
initial_assignments = df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')]['newvalue__c'].unique()
initial_assignments = [clean_id(x) for x in initial_assignments if x is not None and x != '']

# Get subsequent owners (transferred to)
subsequent_owners = df[df['is_transfer']]['newvalue__c'].unique()
subsequent_owners = [clean_id(x) for x in subsequent_owners if x is not None and x != '']

# Get agents who transferred away (these may not necessarily appear as initial or subsequent)
transferring_agents = df[df['is_transfer']]['oldvalue__c'].unique()
transferring_agents = [clean_id(x) for x in transferring_agents if x is not None and x != '']

# Combine all agents who handled cases
all_handling_agents = set(initial_assignments) | set(subsequent_owners) | set(transferring_agents)
all_handling_agents.discard('')  # Remove empty string if present
all_handling_agents.discard('None')  # Remove 'None' string if present

# Filter out agents with 0 cases handled
# We need to count cases per agent - both initial assignments and cases transferred to them

# Count initial assignments per agent
initial_counts = df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')]
initial_counts = initial_counts.groupby('newvalue__c').size().reset_index(name='initial_count')
initial_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'initial_count']

# Count subsequent assignments (transferred to) per agent
subsequent_counts = df[df['is_transfer']].groupby('newvalue__c').size().reset_index(name='subsequent_count')
subsequent_counts.columns = ['agent_id', 'subsequent_count']

# Combine counts
case_counts = pd.merge(initial_counts, subsequent_counts, on='agent_id', how='outer')
case_counts['total_cases'] = case_counts['initial_count'].fillna(0) + case_counts['subsequent_count'].fillna(0)

# Filter agents with > 0 cases
df['caseid__c'] = df['caseid__c'].astype(str)
case_counts['agent_id'] = case_counts['agent_id'].astype(str)

# Count unique cases per agent (both initial and subsequent)
cases_by_agent = {}

# Initial assignments
for idx, row in df[(df['oldvalue__c'].isna()) | (df['oldvalue__c'] == 'None') | (df['oldvalue__c'] == '')].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
    cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Subsequent assignments
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['newvalue__c'])
    case_id = str(row['caseid__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()
    cases_by_agent[agent].add(case_id)

# Also add agents who only transferred away (they may have never been assigned to in our dataset)
for idx, row in df[df['is_transfer']].iterrows():
    agent = str(row['oldvalue__c'])
    if agent not in cases_by_agent:
        # We don't actually know what cases this agent handled, just that they transferred at least one
        # For the purpose of "handled more than 0 cases", we need to verify if they handled any
        cases_by_agent[agent] = set()

# Filter out invalid IDs
cases_by_agent_clean = {}
for agent, cases in cases_by_agent.items():
    if agent and agent != 'None' and agent != '':
        cases_by_agent_clean[agent] = cases

# Create DataFrame with case counts
case_count_df = pd.DataFrame(
    {'agent_id': agent, 'case_count': len(cases)} 
    for agent, cases in cases_by_agent_clean.items()
)

# Filter agents with more than 0 cases
active_agents = case_count_df[case_count_df['case_count'] > 0]

# Add transfer counts
transfer_counts['agent_id'] = transfer_counts['agent_id'].astype(str)

# Merge with active agents
result_df = pd.merge(active_agents, transfer_counts, on='agent_id', how='left')

# Fill NaN transfer counts with 0
result_df['transfer_count'] = result_df['transfer_count'].fillna(0)

# Sort by transfer count and get the agent with the fewest transfers
result_df = result_df.sort_values(['transfer_count', 'agent_id'])

fewest_transfers_agent = result_df.iloc[0]

# Prepare result to include full analysis for debugging
result = {
    'agent_with_fewest_transfers': fewest_transfers_agent['agent_id'],
    'transfer_count': int(fewest_transfers_agent['transfer_count']),
    'cases_handled': int(fewest_transfers_agent['case_count']),
    'total_agents_active': len(active_agents)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))"}
